{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Modern Defence, Main Line| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd| |nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|bd|pd|= | | | | | | |pd| |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pl|pl| | | |= | | |nl| | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} ==Modern Defence, Main Line== This the main line of the Modern Defence. Here Black has allowed White to have a classical pawn duo on e4 and d4. Black must have a plan to attack back or they will be steamrolled. The most common reply is [[/3...d6/]]. Here Black attempts to undermine the center through a hypermodern approach. A more passive approach is [[/3...c6/]] while a risky approach is [[/3...c5/]]. == Theory table == {{ChessTable}} '''1. e4 g6 2. d4 Bg7 3. Nc3''' {| cellpadding="4" cellspacing="0" |+ ! 3 ! 4 |- | …
d6 | Be3
a6 | {{chesspunc|equal}} |- | …
c6 | Nf3 | {{chesspunc|equal}} |}
title: Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...g6/2. d4/2...Bg7/3. Nc3
